diagrams.title: Builder Pattern - Class Diagram
diagrams.description: Step-by-step construction of the Builder pattern showing Product, Builder, Director, and Client.

steps.label: Product
steps.description: The complex object being constructed with many fields
steps.diagram:
classDiagram
    class Meal {
        -bun: String
        -patties: int
        -cheese: String
        -sauce: String
        -size: String
        -price: double
        +toString() String
    }
    note for Meal "Complex object with\nmany optional fields"

steps.label: + Builder
steps.description: The Builder provides a fluent API to construct the Product step-by-step
steps.diagram:
classDiagram
    class Meal {
        -bun: String
        -patties: int
        -cheese: String
        -sauce: String
        -size: String
        -price: double
        +toString() String
    }
    class MealBuilder {
        -bun: String
        -patties: int
        -cheese: String
        -sauce: String
        -size: String
        -price: double
        +setBun(bun: String) MealBuilder
        +setPatties(n: int) MealBuilder
        +setCheese(cheese: String) MealBuilder
        +setSauce(sauce: String) MealBuilder
        +setSize(size: String) MealBuilder
        +setPrice(price: double) MealBuilder
        +build() Meal
    }
    MealBuilder ..> Meal : creates
    note for MealBuilder "Fluent API: each setter\nreturns 'this' for chaining"

steps.label: + Director
steps.description: The Director encapsulates common build recipes
steps.diagram:
classDiagram
    class Meal {
        -bun: String
        -patties: int
        -cheese: String
        -sauce: String
        -size: String
        -price: double
        +toString() String
    }
    class MealBuilder {
        -bun: String
        -patties: int
        +setBun(bun: String) MealBuilder
        +setPatties(n: int) MealBuilder
        +setCheese(cheese: String) MealBuilder
        +setSauce(sauce: String) MealBuilder
        +setSize(size: String) MealBuilder
        +setPrice(price: double) MealBuilder
        +build() Meal
    }
    class MealRecipes {
        +kidsMeal()$ Meal
        +gourmetBurger()$ Meal
        +veggieWrap()$ Meal
    }
    MealBuilder ..> Meal : creates
    MealRecipes ..> MealBuilder : uses
    note for MealRecipes "Director knows WHAT to build\nBuilder knows HOW to build"

steps.label: + Client
steps.description: Client uses either the Director for presets or Builder directly for custom builds
steps.diagram:
classDiagram
    class Meal {
        -bun: String
        -patties: int
        -cheese: String
        -sauce: String
        -size: String
        -price: double
        +toString() String
    }
    class MealBuilder {
        +setBun(bun: String) MealBuilder
        +setPatties(n: int) MealBuilder
        +setCheese(cheese: String) MealBuilder
        +setSauce(sauce: String) MealBuilder
        +setSize(size: String) MealBuilder
        +setPrice(price: double) MealBuilder
        +build() Meal
    }
    class MealRecipes {
        +kidsMeal()$ Meal
        +gourmetBurger()$ Meal
        +veggieWrap()$ Meal
    }
    class OrderService {
        +placeOrder(type: String) void
    }
    MealBuilder ..> Meal : creates
    MealRecipes ..> MealBuilder : uses
    OrderService --> MealRecipes : uses presets
    OrderService --> MealBuilder : or builds custom

diagrams.title: Builder with Director - Class Diagram
diagrams.description: Focus on the Director pattern: how the Director orchestrates the Builder to produce different configurations.

steps.label: Builder Interface
steps.description: Abstract builder interface that all concrete builders implement
steps.diagram:
classDiagram
    class MealBuilder {
        <<interface>>
        +setBun(bun: String) MealBuilder
        +setPatties(n: int) MealBuilder
        +setCheese(cheese: String) MealBuilder
        +setSauce(sauce: String) MealBuilder
        +setSize(size: String) MealBuilder
        +build() Meal
    }
    note for MealBuilder "Interface allows\nswappable builders"

steps.label: + Concrete Builders
steps.description: Different builders can produce different representations
steps.diagram:
classDiagram
    class MealBuilder {
        <<interface>>
        +setBun(bun: String) MealBuilder
        +setPatties(n: int) MealBuilder
        +setCheese(cheese: String) MealBuilder
        +setSauce(sauce: String) MealBuilder
        +setSize(size: String) MealBuilder
        +build() Meal
    }
    class StandardMealBuilder {
        +setBun(bun: String) MealBuilder
        +setPatties(n: int) MealBuilder
        +setCheese(cheese: String) MealBuilder
        +setSauce(sauce: String) MealBuilder
        +setSize(size: String) MealBuilder
        +build() Meal
    }
    class HealthyMealBuilder {
        +setBun(bun: String) MealBuilder
        +setPatties(n: int) MealBuilder
        +setCheese(cheese: String) MealBuilder
        +setSauce(sauce: String) MealBuilder
        +setSize(size: String) MealBuilder
        +build() Meal
    }
    MealBuilder <|.. StandardMealBuilder
    MealBuilder <|.. HealthyMealBuilder

steps.label: + Director with Recipes
steps.description: Director uses any builder to construct pre-defined meals
steps.diagram:
classDiagram
    class MealBuilder {
        <<interface>>
        +setBun(bun: String) MealBuilder
        +setPatties(n: int) MealBuilder
        +setCheese(cheese: String) MealBuilder
        +setSauce(sauce: String) MealBuilder
        +setSize(size: String) MealBuilder
        +build() Meal
    }
    class StandardMealBuilder {
        +build() Meal
    }
    class HealthyMealBuilder {
        +build() Meal
    }
    class MealRecipes {
        -builder: MealBuilder
        +MealRecipes(builder: MealBuilder)
        +kidsMeal() Meal
        +gourmetBurger() Meal
        +veggieWrap() Meal
    }
    MealBuilder <|.. StandardMealBuilder
    MealBuilder <|.. HealthyMealBuilder
    MealRecipes --> MealBuilder : delegates to
    note for MealRecipes "Same recipe, different builder\n= different representation"

diagrams.title: Builder Flow - Sequence Diagram
diagrams.description: Runtime flow: Client uses Builder to construct a Meal step-by-step, then calls build().

steps.label: Direct Builder Usage
steps.description: Client constructs a Meal directly using the Builder fluent API
steps.diagram:
sequenceDiagram
    participant C as Client
    participant B as Meal.Builder
    participant M as Meal

    C->>B: new Builder()
    activate B
    C->>B: setBun("Sesame bun")
    B-->>C: this (Builder)
    C->>B: setPatties(2)
    B-->>C: this (Builder)
    C->>B: setCheese("Cheddar")
    B-->>C: this (Builder)
    C->>B: setSauce("BBQ")
    B-->>C: this (Builder)
    C->>B: setSize("Large")
    B-->>C: this (Builder)
    C->>B: build()
    B->>B: validate fields
    B->>M: new Meal(this)
    activate M
    M-->>B: meal instance
    deactivate M
    B-->>C: Meal (immutable)
    deactivate B
    Note over C: Meal is fully built\nand immutable

steps.label: Director-Mediated Build
steps.description: Director orchestrates the Builder to produce a pre-configured Meal
steps.diagram:
sequenceDiagram
    participant C as Client
    participant D as MealRecipes
    participant B as Meal.Builder
    participant M as Meal

    C->>D: kidsMeal()
    activate D
    D->>B: new Builder()
    activate B
    D->>B: setBun("Small-regular bun")
    D->>B: setPatties(1)
    D->>B: setCheese("American")
    D->>B: setSauce("Ketchup")
    D->>B: setSize("Small")
    D->>B: build()
    B->>B: validate fields
    B->>M: new Meal(this)
    activate M
    M-->>B: meal instance
    deactivate M
    B-->>D: Meal
    deactivate B
    D-->>C: Meal (kids)
    deactivate D
    Note over C: Client never touches\nthe Builder directly

steps.label: Validation Failure
steps.description: What happens when build() detects invalid state
steps.diagram:
sequenceDiagram
    participant C as Client
    participant B as Meal.Builder

    C->>B: new Builder()
    activate B
    C->>B: setPatties(1)
    B-->>C: this (Builder)
    C->>B: build()
    B->>B: validate: bun is null!
    B--xC: IllegalStateException
    deactivate B
    Note over C: build() is the\nsingle validation gate\nNo partial objects leak
